_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that historians interpret significant 20th-century developments in Australian society in different ways, and why these differences exist.

_source.w: We are learning that historians interpret significant 20th-century developments in Australian society in different ways, and why these differences exist.
y: Understanding different interpretations helps us think critically about the stories told about Australia's past.
Interpretations change as societies change — what was once accepted may later be questioned.
This skill teaches us to look for evidence, consider perspectives, and form our own informed views.
eg: Comparing how the White Australia Policy is interpreted today versus how it was viewed at the time.
Exploring how the significance of the 1967 referendum is interpreted differently by different groups.
Investigating how Australia's involvement in World War I is commemorated and interpreted.
Discussing why some people see the end of the White Australia Policy as a turning point while others focus on earlier or later changes.
Examining how historians' interpretations of the stolen generations have changed over time.
Analysing two different accounts of the same event and explaining why they differ.
